1	# Analyse de l'intégration Codex (macOS)
2	
3	## Contexte
4	Comprendre comment le plugin `plugins/codex/` s'authentifie sur macOS et comment il récupère les statistiques d'usage.
5	
6	## Fichiers clés
7	- `plugins/codex/plugin.js` — logique du plugin (auth, refresh, fetch usage)
8	- `plugins/codex/plugin.json` — manifest
9	- `plugins/codex/plugin.test.js` — tests, formes de payloads attendues
10	- `src-tauri/src/plugin_engine/host_api.rs` — exposition côté Rust (FS, keychain, env, ccusage runner)
11	
12	---
13	
14	## 1. Authentification (macOS)
15	
16	Le plugin **ne fait pas d'OAuth lui-même** : il **réutilise les credentials locaux** déjà créés par le Codex CLI.
17	
18	### Stratégie de résolution (cascade)
19	Définie en `plugins/codex/plugin.js:1-8` :
20	```
21	CONFIG_AUTH_PATHS = ["~/.config/codex", "~/.codex"]
22	KEYCHAIN_SERVICE  = "Codex Auth"
23	```
24	
25	Ordre de lookup (`resolveAuthPaths` ligne 70 + `loadAuthFromKeychain` ligne 88) :
26	1. Si `$CODEX_HOME` est défini → `$CODEX_HOME/auth.json`
27	2. Sinon : `~/.config/codex/auth.json` puis `~/.codex/auth.json` (legacy)
28	3. Fallback : **macOS Keychain**, service `"Codex Auth"`, lu via `ctx.host.keychain.readGenericPassword(...)`
29	
30	### Pont vers le Keychain (Rust)
31	`src-tauri/src/plugin_engine/host_api.rs:74-124` exécute la commande système :
32	- Lecture : `security find-generic-password -s "Codex Auth" -w`
33	- Écriture : `security add-generic-password -U -s "Codex Auth" -w <value>`
34	
35	Les payloads keychain peuvent être hex-encodés (préfixe `0x`) ; `decodeHexUtf8` (ligne 30) gère ce cas.
36	
37	### Forme du token
38	Structure attendue (`hasTokenLikeAuth` ligne 81) :
39	```json
40	{ "tokens": { "access_token", "refresh_token", "id_token", "account_id" },
41	  "last_refresh": "..." }
42	```
43	Une clé alternative `OPENAI_API_KEY` est aussi acceptée.
44	
45	### Refresh automatique
46	- Constante `REFRESH_AGE_MS = 8 jours` (ligne 8)
47	- Au-delà → POST `https://auth.openai.com/oauth/token` avec `grant_type=refresh_token` et `client_id=app_EMoamEEZ73f0CkXaXp7hrann` (lignes 6, 163-246)
48	- Token rafraîchi ré-écrit à la source (fichier ou keychain)
49	
50	---
51	
52	## 2. Récupération des statistiques
53	
54	### Source principale : API ChatGPT
55	- **Endpoint** : `GET https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/wham/usage` (ligne 7)
56	- **Headers** (lignes 248-263) :
57	  - `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`
58	  - `ChatGPT-Account-Id: <account_id>` si présent
59	  - `User-Agent: OpenUsage`
60	
61	### Source secondaire : ccusage runner
62	- Appelé via `ctx.host.ccusage.query` (lignes 298-327)
63	- Sous-processus Rust (`host_api.rs:1390-1962`) qui parse les **fichiers de sessions/logs locaux** Codex
64	- Fenêtre de 30 jours (`since: "YYYYMMDD"`, `provider: "codex"`)
65	- Fournit la consommation **tokens / coût USD** par jour
66	
67	---
68	
69	## 3. Typologies de consommation
70	
71	Le plugin distingue **trois modèles** :
72	
73	### A. Rate limits (fenêtres glissantes) — `plugin.js:494-596`
74	Réponse `rate_limit` de l'API wham :
75	- **`primary_window`** : fenêtre **5 heures** (session active)
76	- **`secondary_window`** : fenêtre **7 jours** glissants
77	- **`code_review_rate_limit.primary_window`** : fenêtre **7 jours** dédiée Code Review
78	- **`additional_rate_limits[]`** : limites par modèle (ex. "GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark")
79	
80	Champs : `used_percent`, `reset_after_seconds`, `reset_at`.
81	
82	### B. Crédits (solde fixe) — `plugin.js:597-611`
83	- Lu depuis le header `x-codex-credits-balance` ou le body `credits.balance`
84	- Limite affichée : 1000 crédits, solde restant tracké
85	
86	### C. Tokens / coût (pay-as-you-go local) — `plugin.js:621-669`
87	- Issu du runner ccusage (parsing logs locaux)
88	- `totalTokens`, `totalCost` (USD), agrégation 30 jours
89	
90	### Mapping de plan (`plugin.js:275-281`)
91	- `prolite` → "Pro 5x"
92	- `pro` → "Pro 10x"
93	- Autres : formaté via `ctx.fmt.planLabel`
94	
95	---
96	
97	## Synthèse
98	- **Auth macOS** : aucun OAuth interactif ; le plugin pioche dans `~/.codex/auth.json`, `~/.config/codex/auth.json`, ou le **Keychain** (`Codex Auth`), avec refresh automatique du token au bout de 8 jours.
99	- **Stats** : appel HTTP authentifié à `chatgpt.com/backend-api/wham/usage` + parsing des logs locaux via le runner `ccusage`.
100	- **Trois typologies de conso** : rate-limits glissants (5h / 7j / 7j Code Review / par-modèle), solde de **crédits**, et **tokens/coût** historisés sur 30 jours.
101	
102	## Références
103	- `rg -n "wham/usage|Codex Auth|CODEX_HOME" plugins/codex/`
104	- `plugins/codex/plugin.test.js` pour les exemples de payloads
105	
106	---
107	
108	## 4. Validation en conditions réelles (plan Plus, macOS)
109	
110	Test effectué le 2026-04-24 avec un `~/.codex/auth.json` existant (le chemin `~/.config/codex/auth.json` n'existait pas → c'est bien le **legacy path** qui a été retenu par la cascade).
111	
112	### 4.1 Structure réelle de `~/.codex/auth.json`
113	```json
114	{
115	  "OPENAI_API_KEY": "...",
116	  "auth_mode": "...",
117	  "tokens": {
118	    "access_token": "...",
119	    "id_token": "...",
120	    "refresh_token": "...",
121	    "account_id": "user-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
122	  },
123	  "last_refresh": "2026-04-24T13:09:59.613245Z"
124	}
125	```
126	> À noter : les champs `OPENAI_API_KEY` et `auth_mode` n'apparaissent pas dans le code du plugin mais sont bien présents dans le fichier généré par le CLI. Le plugin n'en utilise que `tokens.*` et `last_refresh`.
127	
128	### 4.2 Appel réel
129	```bash
130	TOKEN=$(jq -r '.tokens.access_token' ~/.codex/auth.json)
131	ACCOUNT=$(jq -r '.tokens.account_id' ~/.codex/auth.json)
132	curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
133	     -H "ChatGPT-Account-Id: $ACCOUNT" \
134	     -H "User-Agent: OpenUsage" \
135	     https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/wham/usage
136	```
137	→ **HTTP 200**, `content-type: application/json`.
138	
139	### 4.3 Payload réel — compte **ChatGPT Plus**, aucune activité Codex
140	```json
141	{
142	  "user_id": "user-…",
143	  "account_id": "user-…",
144	  "email": "…",
145	  "plan_type": "plus",
146	  "rate_limit": {
147	    "allowed": true,
148	    "limit_reached": false,
149	    "primary_window": {
150	      "used_percent": 0,
151	      "limit_window_seconds": 18000,
152	      "reset_after_seconds": 18000,
153	      "reset_at": 1777054501
154	    },
155	    "secondary_window": {
156	      "used_percent": 0,
157	      "limit_window_seconds": 604800,
158	      "reset_after_seconds": 604800,
159	      "reset_at": 1777641301
160	    }
161	  },
162	  "code_review_rate_limit": null,
163	  "additional_rate_limits": null,
164	  "credits": {
165	    "has_credits": false,
166	    "unlimited": false,
167	    "overage_limit_reached": false,
168	    "balance": "0",
169	    "approx_local_messages": [0, 0],
170	    "approx_cloud_messages": [0, 0]
171	  },
172	  "spend_control": { "reached": false },
173	  "rate_limit_reached_type": null,
174	  "promo": null,
175	  "referral_beacon": null
176	}
177	```
178	
179	Clés non documentées initialement mais présentes dans la réponse :
180	- `user_id`, `email`, `account_id`
181	- `spend_control.reached` (booléen — contrôle de dépense global)
182	- `rate_limit_reached_type` (type du rate-limit atteint, sinon null)
183	- `promo`, `referral_beacon`
184	- `credits.has_credits`, `credits.unlimited`, `credits.overage_limit_reached`
185	- `credits.approx_local_messages` / `approx_cloud_messages` (tuples `[used, limit]` approximatifs en nombre de messages locaux/cloud)
186	- `rate_limit.allowed`, `rate_limit.limit_reached`
187	- `limit_window_seconds` dans chaque window (primary = 18 000 s = 5 h ; secondary = 604 800 s = 7 j)
188	
189	### 4.4 Observations spécifiques au plan Plus
190	- Seulement 2 fenêtres exploitables : **Session (5 h)** et **Weekly (7 j)**.
191	- `code_review_rate_limit: null` → **pas de quota Code Review** (réservé aux plans supérieurs).
192	- `additional_rate_limits: null` → **pas de limite par modèle** (ex. Spark absent).
193	- `credits.balance: "0"` + `has_credits: false` → **pas de crédits pay-as-you-go actifs**.
194	- Header `x-codex-credits-balance` **non retourné** sur ce plan.
195	
196	---
197	
198	## 5. Exemples de payloads enrichis (issus de `plugin.test.js`)
199	
200	### 5.1 Compte avec Code Review + Credits (`plugin.test.js:616-625`)
201	```json
202	{
203	  "rate_limit": {
204	    "primary_window":   { "used_percent": 10, "reset_after_seconds": 60 },
205	    "secondary_window": { "used_percent": 20, "reset_after_seconds": 120 }
206	  },
207	  "code_review_rate_limit": {
208	    "primary_window": { "used_percent": 15, "reset_after_seconds": 90 }
209	  },
210	  "credits": { "balance": 500 }
211	}
212	```
213	→ le plugin produit les lignes `Session`, `Weekly`, `Reviews`, `Credits (used=500)`.
214	
215	### 5.2 Compte avec limite par modèle — "Spark" (`plugin.test.js:770-795`)
216	```json
217	{
218	  "rate_limit": {
219	    "primary_window":   { "used_percent": 5,  "reset_after_seconds": 60 },
220	    "secondary_window": { "used_percent": 10, "reset_after_seconds": 120 }
221	  },
222	  "additional_rate_limits": [
223	    {
224	      "limit_name": "GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark",
225	      "metered_feature": "codex_bengalfox",
226	      "rate_limit": {
227	        "primary_window": {
228	          "used_percent": 25,
229	          "limit_window_seconds": 18000,
230	          "reset_after_seconds": 3600,
231	          "reset_at": 1700003600
232	        },
233	        "secondary_window": {
234	          "used_percent": 40,
235	          "limit_window_seconds": 604800,
236	          "reset_after_seconds": 86400,
237	          "reset_at": 1700086400
238	        }
239	      }
240	    }
241	  ]
242	}
243	```
244	→ produit deux lignes `Spark` (5 h) et `Spark Weekly` (7 j). Si `limit_name` est vide, fallback au label `Model` (`plugin.test.js:846`).
245	
246	### 5.3 Headers observables
247	- `x-codex-credits-balance` : solde de crédits (si `credits` absent du body)
248	- `x-codex-primary-used-percent` : pourcentage d'usage primary (fallback)
249	
250	### 5.4 Mapping des `plan_type`
251	| API | Label affiché |
252	|---|---|
253	| `plus` | `Plus` (format générique via `ctx.fmt.planLabel`) |
254	| `prolite` | `Pro 5x` |
255	| `pro` | `Pro 10x` |
256	| autre | formaté générique |
257

## 2. Récupération des statistiques

### Source principale : API ChatGPT
- **Endpoint** : `GET https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/wham/usage` (ligne 7)
- **Headers** (lignes 248-263) :
  - `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`
  - `ChatGPT-Account-Id: <account_id>` si présent
  - `User-Agent: OpenUsage`

### Source secondaire : ccusage runner
- Appelé via `ctx.host.ccusage.query` (lignes 298-327)
- Sous-processus Rust (`host_api.rs:1390-1962`) qui parse les **fichiers de sessions/logs locaux** Codex
- Fenêtre de 30 jours (`since: "YYYYMMDD"`, `provider: "codex"`)
- Fournit la consommation **tokens / coût USD** par jour

---

## 3. Typologies de consommation

Le plugin distingue **trois modèles** :

### A. Rate limits (fenêtres glissantes) — `plugin.js:494-596`
Réponse `rate_limit` de l'API wham :
- **`primary_window`** : fenêtre **5 heures** (session active)
- **`secondary_window`** : fenêtre **7 jours** glissants
- **`code_review_rate_limit.primary_window`** : fenêtre **7 jours** dédiée Code Review
- **`additional_rate_limits[]`** : limites par modèle (ex. "GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark")

Champs : `used_percent`, `reset_after_seconds`, `reset_at`.

### B. Crédits (solde fixe) — `plugin.js:597-611`
- Lu depuis le header `x-codex-credits-balance` ou le body `credits.balance`
- Limite affichée : 1000 crédits, solde restant tracké

### C. Tokens / coût (pay-as-you-go local) — `plugin.js:621-669`
- Issu du runner ccusage (parsing logs locaux)
- `totalTokens`, `totalCost` (USD), agrégation 30 jours

### Mapping de plan (`plugin.js:275-281`)
- `prolite` → "Pro 5x"
- `pro` → "Pro 10x"
- Autres : formaté via `ctx.fmt.planLabel`

---

## Synthèse
- **Auth macOS** : aucun OAuth interactif ; le plugin pioche dans `~/.codex/auth.json`, `~/.config/codex/auth.json`, ou le **Keychain** (`Codex Auth`), avec refresh automatique du token au bout de 8 jours.
- **Stats** : appel HTTP authentifié à `chatgpt.com/backend-api/wham/usage` + parsing des logs locaux via le runner `ccusage`.
- **Trois typologies de conso** : rate-limits glissants (5h / 7j / 7j Code Review / par-modèle), solde de **crédits**, et **tokens/coût** historisés sur 30 jours.

## Références
- `rg -n "wham/usage|Codex Auth|CODEX_HOME" plugins/codex/`
- `plugins/codex/plugin.test.js` pour les exemples de payloads

---

## 4. Validation en conditions réelles (plan Plus, macOS)

Test effectué le 2026-04-24 avec un `~/.codex/auth.json` existant (le chemin `~/.config/codex/auth.json` n'existait pas → c'est bien le **legacy path** qui a été retenu par la cascade).

### 4.1 Structure réelle de `~/.codex/auth.json`
```json
{
  "OPENAI_API_KEY": "...",
  "auth_mode": "...",
  "tokens": {
    "access_token": "...",
    "id_token": "...",
    "refresh_token": "...",
    "account_id": "user-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
  },
  "last_refresh": "2026-04-24T13:09:59.613245Z"
}
```
> À noter : les champs `OPENAI_API_KEY` et `auth_mode` n'apparaissent pas dans le code du plugin mais sont bien présents dans le fichier généré par le CLI. Le plugin n'en utilise que `tokens.*` et `last_refresh`.

### 4.2 Appel réel
```bash
TOKEN=$(jq -r '.tokens.access_token' ~/.codex/auth.json)
ACCOUNT=$(jq -r '.tokens.account_id' ~/.codex/auth.json)
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
     -H "ChatGPT-Account-Id: $ACCOUNT" \
     -H "User-Agent: OpenUsage" \
     https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/wham/usage
```
→ **HTTP 200**, `content-type: application/json`.

### 4.3 Payload réel — compte **ChatGPT Plus**, aucune activité Codex
```json
{
  "user_id": "user-…",
  "account_id": "user-…",
  "email": "…",
  "plan_type": "plus",
  "rate_limit": {
    "allowed": true,
    "limit_reached": false,
    "primary_window": {
      "used_percent": 0,
      "limit_window_seconds": 18000,
      "reset_after_seconds": 18000,
      "reset_at": 1777054501
    },
    "secondary_window": {
      "used_percent": 0,
      "limit_window_seconds": 604800,
      "reset_after_seconds": 604800,
      "reset_at": 1777641301
    }
  },
  "code_review_rate_limit": null,
  "additional_rate_limits": null,
  "credits": {
    "has_credits": false,
    "unlimited": false,
    "overage_limit_reached": false,
    "balance": "0",
    "approx_local_messages": [0, 0],
    "approx_cloud_messages": [0, 0]
  },
  "spend_control": { "reached": false },
  "rate_limit_reached_type": null,
  "promo": null,
  "referral_beacon": null
}
```

Clés non documentées initialement mais présentes dans la réponse :
- `user_id`, `email`, `account_id`
- `spend_control.reached` (booléen — contrôle de dépense global)
- `rate_limit_reached_type` (type du rate-limit atteint, sinon null)
- `promo`, `referral_beacon`
- `credits.has_credits`, `credits.unlimited`, `credits.overage_limit_reached`
- `credits.approx_local_messages` / `approx_cloud_messages` (tuples `[used, limit]` approximatifs en nombre de messages locaux/cloud)
- `rate_limit.allowed`, `rate_limit.limit_reached`
- `limit_window_seconds` dans chaque window (primary = 18 000 s = 5 h ; secondary = 604 800 s = 7 j)

### 4.4 Observations spécifiques au plan Plus
- Seulement 2 fenêtres exploitables : **Session (5 h)** et **Weekly (7 j)**.
- `code_review_rate_limit: null` → **pas de quota Code Review** (réservé aux plans supérieurs).
- `additional_rate_limits: null` → **pas de limite par modèle** (ex. Spark absent).
- `credits.balance: "0"` + `has_credits: false` → **pas de crédits pay-as-you-go actifs**.
- Header `x-codex-credits-balance` **non retourné** sur ce plan.

---

## 5. Exemples de payloads enrichis (issus de `plugin.test.js`)

### 5.1 Compte avec Code Review + Credits (`plugin.test.js:616-625`)
```json
{
  "rate_limit": {
    "primary_window":   { "used_percent": 10, "reset_after_seconds": 60 },
    "secondary_window": { "used_percent": 20, "reset_after_seconds": 120 }
  },
  "code_review_rate_limit": {
    "primary_window": { "used_percent": 15, "reset_after_seconds": 90 }
  },
  "credits": { "balance": 500 }
}
```
→ le plugin produit les lignes `Session`, `Weekly`, `Reviews`, `Credits (used=500)`.

### 5.2 Compte avec limite par modèle — "Spark" (`plugin.test.js:770-795`)
```json
{
  "rate_limit": {
    "primary_window":   { "used_percent": 5,  "reset_after_seconds": 60 },
    "secondary_window": { "used_percent": 10, "reset_after_seconds": 120 }
  },
  "additional_rate_limits": [
    {
      "limit_name": "GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark",
      "metered_feature": "codex_bengalfox",
      "rate_limit": {
        "primary_window": {
          "used_percent": 25,
          "limit_window_seconds": 18000,
          "reset_after_seconds": 3600,
          "reset_at": 1700003600
        },
        "secondary_window": {
          "used_percent": 40,
          "limit_window_seconds": 604800,
          "reset_after_seconds": 86400,
          "reset_at": 1700086400
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```
→ produit deux lignes `Spark` (5 h) et `Spark Weekly` (7 j). Si `limit_name` est vide, fallback au label `Model` (`plugin.test.js:846`).

### 5.3 Headers observables
- `x-codex-credits-balance` : solde de crédits (si `credits` absent du body)
- `x-codex-primary-used-percent` : pourcentage d'usage primary (fallback)

### 5.4 Mapping des `plan_type`
| API | Label affiché |
|---|---|
| `plus` | `Plus` (format générique via `ctx.fmt.planLabel`) |
| `prolite` | `Pro 5x` |
| `pro` | `Pro 10x` |
| autre | formaté générique |

# Markdown authoring rules

## Language: English only

All Markdown files in this repository MUST be written in English, regardless of the conversation language. This includes `CLAUDE.md`, every file under `docs/`, `README.md`, and any new `*.md`.

- When creating a new Markdown file, write it in English from the start even if the user's prompt is in another language.
- When editing an existing Markdown file that contains non-English prose, translate the edited sections to English instead of preserving mixed languages.
- Code blocks, file paths, commands, and identifiers keep their original form — this rule applies to natural-language prose only.

## Write for longevity

Markdown in this repository is long-lived context. Optimize each file so it does not need to be rewritten as the code evolves. Avoid anything that ties documentation to a specific moment in time or to renamable artifacts:

- Do NOT reference specific commit SHAs, PR numbers, or branch names.
- Do NOT describe the project as being in a transient state ("currently a Hello World", "not yet implemented", "WIP"). If a feature does not exist, simply do not document it.
- Do NOT speculate about intent or future direction ("the name suggests…", "will eventually…"). Document what the code actually does today in terms that remain true tomorrow.
- Do NOT list every file, symbol, or dependency when a structural description is enough — exhaustive lists drift out of date quickly.
- Avoid naming specific files, types, or symbols that could be renamed or moved. Prefer describing roles and responsibilities ("the SwiftUI entry point", "the package manifest") over fixed identifiers. When a path is truly stable because a tool mandates it (e.g. `Package.swift` for Swift Package Manager, `.claude/rules/` for Claude Code), it is fine to use it.
- Do NOT duplicate information that already lives authoritatively in code or tool manifests (version numbers, platform minimums, target lists). Point readers at the source of truth instead of mirroring it.

When in doubt, ask: "Will this sentence still be accurate after the next three features ship, or after a plausible rename?" If not, rephrase or remove it.

## Single source of truth across Markdown files

When two Markdown files cover the same topic, exactly one is the authoritative spec; the other references it and adds only what is genuinely its own (workflow steps, scaffolding procedure, agent phases, etc.).

This applies in particular to these pairs:

- `docs/<topic>.md` (spec) ↔ `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (skill that implements it)
- `docs/<topic>.md` (spec) ↔ `.claude/agents/<name>.md` (agent prompt that uses it)
- `docs/<topic>.md` (spec) ↔ `.claude/commands/<name>.md` (slash command that triggers it)

Rules:

- Templates, file formats, naming conventions, and directory layouts belong in the spec, NOT in the operational artifact.
- The operational artifact MUST start with a one-line pointer ("Read `docs/<topic>.md` first; it defines X, Y, Z.") and MUST include a conflict-resolution line ("If this artifact disagrees with `docs/<topic>.md`, the spec wins.").
- Before adding concrete content (a template block, a format string, a list of sections) to an operational artifact, check whether that content already exists in a `docs/` spec. If it does, reference it instead of copying it.

## Exception: `roadmap/`

Files under `roadmap/` intentionally describe planned, not-yet-implemented work. The "Write for longevity" rules above (no transient state, no speculation about future direction) do NOT apply there — a roadmap is precisely a record of intent and ordering that will evolve as features ship. The English-only rule still applies. See `docs/ROADMAP.md` for the roadmap file conventions.

1	# Architecture
2	
3	An executable Swift Package produces a **menubar-only** macOS application: no Dock icon, no main window, and all interaction flows through a single menu bar item.
4	
5	Invariants worth preserving when modifying the codebase:
6	
7	- The app sets its activation policy to `.accessory` at startup. Removing this call makes macOS show a Dock icon and breaks the menubar-only contract.

# Architecture

An executable Swift Package produces a **menubar-only** macOS application: no Dock icon, no main window, and all interaction flows through a single menu bar item.

Invariants worth preserving when modifying the codebase:

- The app sets its activation policy to `.accessory` at startup. Removing this call makes macOS show a Dock icon and breaks the menubar-only contract.
- The UI is exposed through an AppKit `NSStatusItem` owned by `AppDelegate`, not SwiftUI's `MenuBarExtra`. `MenuBarExtra` was dropped after it proved unable to render per-segment colored indicators (template tinting strips colors, mixed HStacks silently truncate). See `docs/SWIFT-MENUBAR.md` before changing anything menu-bar related. User-visible features belong inside the popover's root SwiftUI view — there is intentionally no main `WindowGroup`.

## Package structure

The Swift package is split into a library target and an executable target, plus a test target that exercises the library. The library contains all domain logic; the executable is a thin SwiftUI entry point.

## Usage-fetching pipeline

A `UsageConnector` protocol abstracts vendor-specific API access. Each connector resolves an active account and fetches usage data asynchronously. A connector may also emit additional optional per-model `timeWindow` entries when the upstream API includes them — these travel through the same persistence and display pipeline as the mandatory session and weekly aggregates, and are silently omitted when the API does not provide them. The first concrete implementation targets the Claude API via OAuth tokens stored in the macOS Keychain.

A polling actor periodically invokes all registered connectors in parallel and merges results into a shared JSON file.

## Persistence

Usage data is persisted as a JSON file at `~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/usages.json`. The schema is a top-level `usages` array where each entry is keyed by `(vendor, account)`. A dedicated actor (`UsagesFileManager`) serializes all reads and writes for internal callers. Writes use the system's atomic write facility to prevent partial-file corruption. All public methods also acquire an advisory POSIX `flock` on a dedicated lock file (`usages.json.lock`) before touching the data file, so external readers (widgets, scripts) that do the same can safely coordinate access.

## Display pipeline

A file watcher observes `usages.json` using a hybrid strategy: a kernel `DispatchSource` fires on `.write`/`.delete`/`.rename` events, backed by a polling timer that re-checks on a configurable polling interval to handle atomic replaces and network-mount edge cases. Events are debounced to coalesce rapid successive writes into a single read.

An `@Observable` store (running on `@MainActor`) receives each new file snapshot, decodes it, locates the active Claude account, and formats the session and weekly time-window metrics into the menubar label. A secondary countdown timer periodically refreshes the remaining-time values in the display so they stay current between file changes. When data is unavailable or malformed the label falls back to `"--"`.

When the user clicks the menu bar item, `AppDelegate` toggles an `NSPopover` hosting the root SwiftUI view via `NSHostingController`. The popover renders one card per vendor/account entry, sorted alphabetically by vendor then by account (active accounts first within a vendor). Each card shows time-window metrics (gauge bar, theoretical pace marker, remaining time, next reset date) and pay-as-you-go metrics (amount with currency). An empty state is shown when no entries are available. A footer provides the app name and a Quit shortcut.

The menu bar label itself is rasterised into a non-template `NSImage` by `MenuBarLabelRenderer`, so per-tier dot colors survive macOS's template tinting. Text color is picked from the status button's `effectiveAppearance` (which tracks the menu bar, not the app) and re-rendered whenever that appearance changes.

## Account monitoring

A separate monitoring actor polls the vendor's local config file at a short fixed interval to detect account switches in real time. When a switch is detected, it updates the `isActive` flag on the corresponding persistence entry without waiting for the next usage fetch. This separation keeps account-status latency low without coupling it to the (slower) API polling cadence.

## Preferences

An injectable preferences protocol backed by `UserDefaults` is the single source of truth for user-adjustable runtime behaviour. Two consumers read it: the polling actor (refresh cadence) and the logging subsystem (verbosity). Changes made in the settings window take effect immediately — the poller re-reads the interval on every tick, and the logger resolves its effective level on every log call.

The settings window is a standard SwiftUI `Settings` scene (tabbed, HIG chrome, `Cmd+,`). Because the app uses `.accessory` activation policy, opening the window requires explicitly activating the app before sending the `showSettingsWindow:` action.

Launch-at-login state is reconciled at startup: if the user toggled the entry in System Settings > Login Items, the preferences store is updated to match the system state rather than overriding it.

## Logging

Two log files live under `~/.cache/ai-usages-tracker/`: one for the app lifecycle and poller events, another for connector-specific activity. Log level is configurable via the `AI_TRACKER_LOG_LEVEL` environment variable; when that variable is absent, the level falls back to the user's setting in the preferences window (default: info). Size-based rotation keeps each file under 5 MB with one backup. A retention purge removes entries older than 7 days from all managed log files at startup and every 24 hours, using atomic rewrites so a crash mid-cleanup leaves the previous file intact.

For authoritative details (Swift tools version, platform minimums, target layout), read the package manifest rather than mirroring them here.

1	import Foundation
2	
3	public actor ClaudeCodeConnector: UsageConnector {
4	    nonisolated public let vendor: Vendor = .claude
5	
6	    private let claudeConfigPath: String
7	    private let logger: FileLogger
8	    private let session: URLSession
9	    private let keychainServiceName: String
10	    private let tokenProvider: (@Sendable () async throws -> String)?
11	    private let accountProvider: (@Sendable () -> AccountEmail?)?
12	
13	    private var lastKnownMetrics: [UsageMetric] = []
14	
15	    // Long enough for a cached keychain lookup; short enough to avoid stalling the poller
16	    private static let keychainTimeoutSeconds: Int = 10
17	    // Claude Pro/Max rate-limit policy — durations are fixed by Anthropic and not returned by the API
18	    private static let sessionWindowMinutes = DurationMinutes(rawValue: 300)
19	    private static let weeklyWindowMinutes  = DurationMinutes(rawValue: 10080)
20	
21	    // Actor-isolated: safe without nonisolated(unsafe) since all access is on the actor
22	    private let isoFormatter = ISO8601DateFormatter()
23	    private let isoFormatterFractional: ISO8601DateFormatter = {
24	        let f = ISO8601DateFormatter()
25	        f.formatOptions = [.withInternetDateTime, .withFractionalSeconds]
26	        return f
27	    }()
28	
29	
30	    public init(
31	        claudeConfigPath: String? = nil,
32	        logger: FileLogger = Loggers.claude,
33	        session: URLSession = .shared,
34	        keychainServiceName: String = "Claude Code-credentials",
35	        tokenProvider: (@Sendable () async throws -> String)? = nil,
36	        accountProvider: (@Sendable () -> AccountEmail?)? = nil
37	    ) {
38	        let home = FileManager.default.homeDirectoryForCurrentUser.path
39	        self.claudeConfigPath = claudeConfigPath ?? "\(home)/.claude.json"
40	        self.logger = logger
41	        self.session = session
42	        self.keychainServiceName = keychainServiceName
43	        self.tokenProvider = tokenProvider
44	        self.accountProvider = accountProvider
45	    }
46	
47	    // MARK: - UsageConnector
48	
49	    nonisolated public func resolveActiveAccount() -> AccountEmail? {
50	        if let provider = accountProvider {
51	            return provider()
52	        }
53	        guard let data = FileManager.default.contents(atPath: claudeConfigPath) else {
54	            logger.log(.warning, "Cannot read \(claudeConfigPath)")
55	            return nil
56	        }
57	        do {
58	            let json = try JSONSerialization.jsonObject(with: data) as? [String: Any]
59	            let oauth = json?["oauthAccount"] as? [String: Any]
60	            return (oauth?["emailAddress"] as? String).map(AccountEmail.init(rawValue:))
61	        } catch {
62	            logger.log(.error, "Failed to parse \(claudeConfigPath): \(error)")
63	            return nil
64	        }
65	    }
66	
67	    public func fetchUsages() async throws -> [VendorUsageEntry] {
68	        guard let earlyAccount = resolveActiveAccount() else {
69	            logger.log(.warning, "Cannot resolve active account — skipping fetch")
70	            return [errorEntry(account: "unknown", type: "account_unknown")]
71	        }
72	        logger.log(.info, "Fetching usages for account=\(earlyAccount)")
73	
74	        let token: String
75	        do {
76	            if let provider = tokenProvider {
77	                token = try await provider()
78	            } else {
79	                token = try await fetchOAuthToken()
80	            }
81	        } catch {
82	            logger.log(.error, "Token retrieval failed: \(error)")
83	            return [errorEntry(account: earlyAccount, type: "token_error")]
84	        }
85	
86	        // Re-read at HTTP dispatch time: if the user switched accounts while the token was being
87	        // fetched, the response must be attributed to whoever was active when the request went out.
88	        let account = resolveActiveAccount() ?? earlyAccount
89	
90	        guard let url = URL(string: "https://api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage") else {
91	            return [errorEntry(account: account, type: "api_error")]
92	        }
93	        var request = URLRequest(url: url)
94	        request.setValue("Bearer \(token)", forHTTPHeaderField: "Authorization")
95	        // Required by the OAuth usage endpoint — beta flag must match the rollout date
96	        request.setValue("oauth-2025-04-20", forHTTPHeaderField: "anthropic-beta")
97	        request.timeoutInterval = 5
98	
99	        let data: Data
100	        let response: URLResponse
101	        do {
102	            (data, response) = try await session.data(for: request)
103	        } catch {
104	            logger.log(.error, "API request failed: \(error)")
105	            return [errorEntry(account: account, type: "api_error")]
106	        }
107	
108	        let httpCode = (response as? HTTPURLResponse)?.statusCode ?? -1
109	        logger.log(.info, "API response: HTTP \(httpCode)")
110	
111	        if httpCode == 429 {
112	            let body = String(data: data, encoding: .utf8) ?? "<non-UTF8 body, \(data.count) bytes>"
113	            logger.log(.warning, "API rate-limited (HTTP 429): \(body)")
114	            return [errorEntry(account: account, type: "http_429", isActive: true, preservedMetrics: lastKnownMetrics)]
115	        }
116	
117	        guard let http = response as? HTTPURLResponse, http.statusCode == 200 else {
118	            logger.log(.error, "API returned HTTP \(httpCode)")
119	            return [errorEntry(account: account, type: "http_\(httpCode)")]
120	        }
121	
122	        logger.log(.debug, "API payload: \(Self.maskedPayload(data))")
123	
124	        do {
125	            let usage = try parseAPIResponse(data)
126	            logger.log(.info, "Fetched successfully: session=\(usage.sessionPercent.map { "\($0)%" } ?? "nil") weekly=\(usage.weeklyPercent.map { "\($0)%" } ?? "nil") sonnet=\(usage.sonnetWeekly.map { "\($0.percent)%" } ?? "nil") opus=\(usage.opusWeekly.map { "\($0.percent)%" } ?? "nil")")
127	            // A window is emitted whenever utilization is known, even if resets_at is null
128	            // (fresh account, between cycles). A null reset date surfaces in the UI as "???"
129	            // rather than silently omitting the metric.
130	            var metrics: [UsageMetric] = []
131	            if let percent = usage.sessionPercent {
132	                metrics.append(.timeWindow(
133	                    name: "5h sessions (all models)",
134	                    resetAt: usage.sessionResetAt,
135	                    windowDuration: Self.sessionWindowMinutes,
136	                    usagePercent: UsagePercent(rawValue: percent)
137	                ))
138	            }
139	            if let percent = usage.weeklyPercent {
140	                metrics.append(.timeWindow(
141	                    name: "Weekly (all models)",
142	                    resetAt: usage.weeklyResetAt,
143	                    windowDuration: Self.weeklyWindowMinutes,
144	                    usagePercent: UsagePercent(rawValue: percent)
145	                ))
146	            }
147	            // Per-model weekly metrics are purely additive — absent keys are silently skipped
148	            let perModelDefs: [(metric: ModelWeeklyMetric?, name: String)] = [
149	                (usage.sonnetWeekly, "Weekly Sonnet"),
150	                (usage.opusWeekly, "Weekly Opus"),
151	            ]
152	            for def in perModelDefs {
153	                if let m = def.metric {
154	                    metrics.append(.timeWindow(
155	                        name: def.name,
156	                        resetAt: m.resetAt,
157	                        windowDuration: Self.weeklyWindowMinutes,
158	                        usagePercent: UsagePercent(rawValue: m.percent)
159	                    ))
160	                }
161	            }
162	            lastKnownMetrics = metrics
163	            return [VendorUsageEntry(
164	                vendor: vendor,
165	                account: account,
166	                isActive: true,
167	                lastAcquiredOn: ISODate(date: Date()),
168	                lastError: nil,
169	                metrics: metrics
170	            )]
171	        } catch {
172	            logger.log(.error, "Response parse failed: \(error)")
173	            logger.log(.warning, "Failed payload dump: \(Self.maskedPayload(data))")
174	            return [errorEntry(account: account, type: "parse_error")]
175	        }
176	    }
177	
178	    // MARK: - Token
179	
180	    private func fetchOAuthToken() async throws -> String {
181	        let serviceName = keychainServiceName
182	        return try await withCheckedThrowingContinuation { continuation in
183	            DispatchQueue.global().async {
184	                let process = Process()
185	                let pipe = Pipe()
186	                process.executableURL = URL(fileURLWithPath: "/usr/bin/security")
187	                process.arguments = ["find-generic-password", "-s", serviceName, "-w"]
188	                process.standardOutput = pipe
189	                process.standardError = FileHandle.nullDevice
190	
191	                do {
192	                    try process.run()
193	                } catch {
194	                    continuation.resume(throwing: error)
195	                    return
196	                }
197	
198	                // Keychain may prompt the user or hang if the login keychain is locked
199	                let timedOut = DispatchSemaphore(value: 0)
200	                DispatchQueue.global().asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + .seconds(Self.keychainTimeoutSeconds)) {
201	                    guard process.isRunning else { return }
202	                    process.terminate()
203	                    timedOut.signal()
204	                }
205	
206	                let raw = pipe.fileHandleForReading.readDataToEndOfFile()
207	                process.waitUntilExit()
208	
209	                // Check the flag rather than terminationReason, which may not
210	                // reflect SIGTERM reliably if the process handles the signal
211	                if timedOut.wait(timeout: .now()) == .success {
212	                    continuation.resume(throwing: ConnectorError.keychainTimeout(serviceName: serviceName, timeoutSeconds: Self.keychainTimeoutSeconds))
213	                    return
214	                }
215	                guard process.terminationStatus == 0 else {
216	                    continuation.resume(throwing: ConnectorError.keychainAccessDenied(serviceName: serviceName, exitCode: process.terminationStatus))
217	                    return
218	                }
219	                guard let jsonString = String(data: raw, encoding: .utf8)?.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines),
220	                      !jsonString.isEmpty else {
221	                    continuation.resume(throwing: ConnectorError.keychainEmpty(serviceName: serviceName))
222	                    return
223	                }
224	
225	                guard let jsonData = jsonString.data(using: .utf8) else {
226	                    continuation.resume(throwing: ConnectorError.tokenParseError(rawValue: jsonString))
227	                    return
228	                }
229	                let parsedJSON: [String: Any]
230	                do {
231	                    parsedJSON = try JSONSerialization.jsonObject(with: jsonData) as? [String: Any] ?? [:]
232	                } catch {
233	                    continuation.resume(throwing: ConnectorError.tokenParseError(rawValue: jsonString))
234	                    return
235	                }
236	                guard let oauthDict = parsedJSON["claudeAiOauth"] as? [String: Any],
237	                      let token = oauthDict["accessToken"] as? String,
238	                      !token.isEmpty else {
239	                    continuation.resume(throwing: ConnectorError.tokenParseError(rawValue: jsonString))
240	                    return
241	                }
242	
243	                continuation.resume(returning: token)
244	            }
245	        }
246	    }
247	
248	    // MARK: - Response parsing
249	
250	    private static let sensitiveKeyPatterns = ["token", "key", "secret", "password", "email", "credential"]
251	
252	    private static func maskedPayload(_ data: Data) -> String {
253	        guard let json = try? JSONSerialization.jsonObject(with: data) as? [String: Any] else {
254	            return "<non-JSON, \(data.count) bytes>"
255	        }
256	        let masked = maskSensitiveFields(json)
257	        guard let out = try? JSONSerialization.data(withJSONObject: masked, options: [.sortedKeys]),
258	              let str = String(data: out, encoding: .utf8) else {
259	            return "<serialization failed>"
260	        }
261	        return str
262	    }
263	
264	    private static func maskSensitiveFields(_ dict: [String: Any]) -> [String: Any] {
265	        var result: [String: Any] = [:]
266	        for (key, value) in dict {
267	            let lower = key.lowercased()
268	            if sensitiveKeyPatterns.contains(where: { lower.contains($0) }) {
269	                result[key] = "***"
270	            } else if let nested = value as? [String: Any] {
271	                result[key] = maskSensitiveFields(nested)
272	            } else if let array = value as? [[String: Any]] {
273	                result[key] = array.map { maskSensitiveFields($0) }
274	            } else {
275	                result[key] = value
276	            }
277	        }
278	        return result
279	    }
280	
281	    private struct ModelWeeklyMetric {
282	        let percent: Int
283	        let resetAt: ISODate?
284	    }
285	
286	    private struct ParsedUsage {
287	        // Every window is optional: a freshly provisioned account (or an account
288	        // between reset cycles) returns `utilization: 0, resets_at: null` and must
289	        // not be treated as a parse failure.
290	        let sessionPercent: Int?
291	        let sessionResetAt: ISODate?
292	        let weeklyPercent: Int?
293	        let weeklyResetAt: ISODate?
294	        let sonnetWeekly: ModelWeeklyMetric?
295	        let opusWeekly: ModelWeeklyMetric?
296	    }
297	
298	    private func parseAPIResponse(_ data: Data) throws -> ParsedUsage {
299	        let jsonObject = try JSONSerialization.jsonObject(with: data)
300	        guard let json = jsonObject as? [String: Any] else {
301	            throw ConnectorError.unexpectedAPIFormat(receivedKeys: [])
302	        }
303	        // Unknown top-level keys are ignored by design — the API evolves with experimental
304	        // fields (e.g. `omelette_promotional`, `iguana_necktie`) and we only consume the
305	        // windows we know about. Only a truly malformed payload (no known window at all)
306	        // escalates to an error.
307	        let session = extractOptionalModelMetric(from: json, key: "five_hour")
308	        let weekly = extractOptionalModelMetric(from: json, key: "seven_day")
309	        let sonnet = extractOptionalModelMetric(from: json, key: "seven_day_sonnet")
310	        let opus = extractOptionalModelMetric(from: json, key: "seven_day_opus")
311	
312	        let hasFiveHourBlock = json["five_hour"] is [String: Any]
313	        let hasSevenDayBlock = json["seven_day"] is [String: Any]
314	        if !hasFiveHourBlock, !hasSevenDayBlock {
315	            logger.log(.warning, "No known usage window in payload — top-level keys: \(Array(json.keys))")
316	            throw ConnectorError.unexpectedAPIFormat(receivedKeys: Array(json.keys))
317	        }
318	
319	        return ParsedUsage(
320	            sessionPercent: session.map { Int($0.percent) },
321	            sessionResetAt: session.flatMap { $0.resetAt.map { normalizeISO8601($0) } },
322	            weeklyPercent: weekly.map { Int($0.percent) },
323	            weeklyResetAt: weekly.flatMap { $0.resetAt.map { normalizeISO8601($0) } },
324	            sonnetWeekly: sonnet.map { ModelWeeklyMetric(percent: $0.percent, resetAt: $0.resetAt.map { normalizeISO8601($0) }) },
325	            opusWeekly: opus.map { ModelWeeklyMetric(percent: $0.percent, resetAt: $0.resetAt.map { normalizeISO8601($0) }) }
326	        )
327	    }
328	
329	    /// Every usage window block is optional — a missing key or an unexpected shape is silently
330	    /// skipped. A null `resets_at` (fresh account, between cycles) is kept with `resetAt: nil`
331	    /// so the metric still surfaces in the UI as "???".
332	    private func extractOptionalModelMetric(from json: [String: Any], key: String) -> (percent: Int, resetAt: String?)? {
333	        guard let block = json[key] as? [String: Any] else {
334	            return nil
335	        }
336	        guard let util = block["utilization"] as? Double else {
337	            logger.log(.debug, "Block '\(key)' present but utilization missing — skipped")
338	            return nil
339	        }
340	        let resetAt = block["resets_at"] as? String
341	        if resetAt == nil {
342	            logger.log(.debug, "Block '\(key)' has null resets_at — metric emitted without reset date")
343	        }
344	        return (percent: Int(util.rounded()), resetAt: resetAt)
345	    }
346	
347	    /// Strips sub-second precision from API-provided ISO8601 strings so downstream
348	    /// consumers can rely on the standard formatter (no fractional-seconds option needed).
349	    /// Returns the original string unchanged if it cannot be parsed.
350	    private func normalizeISO8601(_ raw: String) -> ISODate {
351	        if let date = isoFormatterFractional.date(from: raw) ?? isoFormatter.date(from: raw) {
352	            return ISODate(rawValue: isoFormatter.string(from: date))
353	        }
354	        return ISODate(rawValue: raw)
355	    }
356	
357	    // MARK: - Helpers
358	
359	    private func errorEntry(account: AccountEmail, type: String, isActive: Bool = false, preservedMetrics: [UsageMetric] = []) -> VendorUsageEntry {
360	        return VendorUsageEntry(
361	            vendor: vendor,
362	            account: account,
363	            isActive: isActive,
364	            lastAcquiredOn: nil,
365	            lastError: UsageError(timestamp: ISODate(date: Date()), type: type),
366	            metrics: preservedMetrics
367	        )
368	    }
369	}
370	
371	public enum ConnectorError: Error, CustomStringConvertible {
372	    case keychainAccessDenied(serviceName: String, exitCode: Int32)
373	    case keychainEmpty(serviceName: String)
374	    case keychainTimeout(serviceName: String, timeoutSeconds: Int)
375	    case tokenParseError(rawValue: String)
376	    case unexpectedAPIFormat(receivedKeys: [String])
377	
378	    public var description: String {
379	        switch self {
380	        case let .keychainAccessDenied(svc, code):
381	            "Keychain access denied for service '\(svc)' (exit \(code))"
382	        case let .keychainEmpty(svc):
383	            "Keychain item is empty for service '\(svc)'"
384	        case let .keychainTimeout(svc, secs):
385	            "Keychain access timed out after \(secs)s for service '\(svc)'"
386	        case let .tokenParseError(raw):
387	            "Failed to parse OAuth token — raw value preview: '\(raw.prefix(80))'"
388	        case let .unexpectedAPIFormat(keys):
389	            "API response does not match expected format — top-level keys: \(keys)"
390	        }
391	    }
392	}

1	import Foundation
2	
3	// MARK: - Root
4	
5	/// The top-level file model: `{ usages: [...], outages?: [...] }`.
6	///
7	/// `outages` is an optional sibling written by an upstream status-fetching process.
8	/// When no incident is ongoing the upstream writer is expected to drop the key
9	/// entirely; the encoder here honours that convention by emitting nothing when
10	/// the in-memory array is empty.
11	public struct UsagesFile: Codable, Equatable, Sendable {
12	    public var usages: [VendorUsageEntry]
13	    public var outages: [Outage]
14	
15	    public init(usages: [VendorUsageEntry] = [], outages: [Outage] = []) {
16	        self.usages = usages
17	        self.outages = outages
18	    }
19	
20	    /// Grouped view for the UI — vendors without outages are omitted.
21	    public var outagesByVendor: [Vendor: [Outage]] {
22	        Dictionary(grouping: outages, by: \.vendor)
23	    }
24	
25	    private enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
26	        case usages, outages
27	    }
28	
29	    public init(from decoder: Decoder) throws {
30	        let container = try decoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
31	        usages = try container.decodeIfPresent([VendorUsageEntry].self, forKey: .usages) ?? []
32	        outages = try container.decodeIfPresent([Outage].self, forKey: .outages) ?? []
33	    }
34	
35	    public func encode(to encoder: Encoder) throws {
36	        var container = encoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
37	        try container.encode(usages, forKey: .usages)
38	        // Upstream convention: absent key when no ongoing outage — mirror that on write
39	        // so the file never carries a stale empty array between incidents.
40	        if !outages.isEmpty {
41	            try container.encode(outages, forKey: .outages)
42	        }
43	    }
44	}
45	
46	// MARK: - VendorUsageEntry
47	
48	public struct VendorUsageEntry: Codable, Equatable, Sendable, Identifiable {
49	    public var id: String { "\(vendor.rawValue):\(account.rawValue)" }
50	
51	    public let vendor: Vendor
52	    public let account: AccountEmail
53	    public var isActive: Bool
54	    public var lastAcquiredOn: ISODate?
55	    public var lastError: UsageError?
56	    public var metrics: [UsageMetric]
57	
58	    public init(
59	        vendor: Vendor,
60	        account: AccountEmail,
61	        isActive: Bool = false,
62	        lastAcquiredOn: ISODate? = nil,
63	        lastError: UsageError? = nil,
64	        metrics: [UsageMetric] = []
65	    ) {
66	        self.vendor = vendor
67	        self.account = account
68	        self.isActive = isActive
69	        self.lastAcquiredOn = lastAcquiredOn
70	        self.lastError = lastError
71	        self.metrics = metrics
72	    }
73	}
74	
75	// MARK: - Error
76	
77	public struct UsageError: Codable, Equatable, Sendable {
78	    public let timestamp: ISODate
79	    public let type: String
80	
81	    public init(timestamp: ISODate, type: String) {
82	        self.timestamp = timestamp
83	        self.type = type
84	    }
85	}
86	
87	// MARK: - Outage
88	
89	/// An active incident on a vendor's platform, written by an upstream status-fetching process.
90	/// The upstream writer removes entries as incidents resolve, so a missing or empty
91	/// `outages` array means "all clear".
92	public struct Outage: Codable, Equatable, Sendable, Identifiable {
93	    public let vendor: Vendor
94	    public let errorMessage: String
95	    public let severity: OutageSeverity
96	    public let since: ISODate
97	    public let href: URL?
98	
99	    /// Composite id — outages have no stable upstream id in this shape, so we
100	    /// derive one from the fields that together identify a single incident row.
101	    public var id: String {
102	        "\(vendor.rawValue)|\(since.rawValue)|\(href?.absoluteString ?? "")"
103	    }
104	
105	    public init(
106	        vendor: Vendor,
107	        errorMessage: String,
108	        severity: OutageSeverity,
109	        since: ISODate,
110	        href: URL? = nil
111	    ) {
112	        self.vendor = vendor
113	        self.errorMessage = errorMessage
114	        self.severity = severity
115	        self.since = since
116	        self.href = href
117	    }
118	
119	    private enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
120	        case vendor, errorMessage, severity, since, href
121	    }
122	
123	    public init(from decoder: Decoder) throws {
124	        let container = try decoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
125	        vendor = try container.decode(Vendor.self, forKey: .vendor)
126	        errorMessage = try container.decode(String.self, forKey: .errorMessage)
127	        severity = try container.decode(OutageSeverity.self, forKey: .severity)
128	        since = try container.decode(ISODate.self, forKey: .since)
129	        if let hrefString = try container.decodeIfPresent(String.self, forKey: .href) {
130	            guard let parsed = URL(string: hrefString) else {
131	                throw DecodingError.dataCorruptedError(
132	                    forKey: .href, in: container,
133	                    debugDescription: "Invalid URL string: \(hrefString)")
134	            }
135	            href = parsed
136	        } else {
137	            href = nil
138	        }
139	    }
140	}
141	
142	// MARK: - Metric (polymorphic on "type" discriminator)
143	
144	public enum UsageMetric: Codable, Equatable, Sendable {
145	    case timeWindow(name: String, resetAt: ISODate?, windowDuration: DurationMinutes, usagePercent: UsagePercent)
146	    case payAsYouGo(name: String, currentAmount: Double, currency: String)
147	    /// A metric type not yet known to this client; retained for round-trip fidelity.
148	    case unknown(String)
149	
150	    // MARK: Kind
151	
152	    public var kind: MetricKind {
153	        switch self {
154	        case .timeWindow:       .timeWindow
155	        case .payAsYouGo:       .payAsYouGo
156	        case .unknown(let t):   .unknown(t)
157	        }
158	    }
159	
160	    // MARK: Coding
161	
162	    private enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
163	        case type, name, resetAt
164	        case windowDuration = "windowDurationMinutes"
165	        case usagePercent, currentAmount, currency
166	    }
167	
168	    public init(from decoder: Decoder) throws {
169	        let container = try decoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
170	        let metricKind = try container.decode(MetricKind.self, forKey: .type)
171	        switch metricKind {
172	        case .timeWindow:
173	            self = .timeWindow(
174	                name: try container.decode(String.self, forKey: .name),
175	                resetAt: try container.decodeIfPresent(ISODate.self, forKey: .resetAt),
176	                windowDuration: try container.decode(DurationMinutes.self, forKey: .windowDuration),
177	                usagePercent: try container.decode(UsagePercent.self, forKey: .usagePercent)
178	            )
179	        case .payAsYouGo:
180	            self = .payAsYouGo(
181	                name: try container.decode(String.self, forKey: .name),
182	                currentAmount: try container.decode(Double.self, forKey: .currentAmount),
183	                currency: try container.decode(String.self, forKey: .currency)
184	            )
185	        case .unknown(let t):
186	            self = .unknown(t)
187	        }
188	    }
189	
190	    public func encode(to encoder: Encoder) throws {
191	        var container = encoder.container(keyedBy: CodingKeys.self)
192	        switch self {
193	        case let .timeWindow(name, resetAt, windowDuration, usagePercent):
194	            try container.encode(MetricKind.timeWindow, forKey: .type)
195	            try container.encode(name, forKey: .name)
196	            try container.encodeIfPresent(resetAt, forKey: .resetAt)
197	            try container.encode(windowDuration, forKey: .windowDuration)
198	            try container.encode(usagePercent, forKey: .usagePercent)
199	        case let .payAsYouGo(name, currentAmount, currency):
200	            try container.encode(MetricKind.payAsYouGo, forKey: .type)
201	            try container.encode(name, forKey: .name)
202	            try container.encode(currentAmount, forKey: .currentAmount)
203	            try container.encode(currency, forKey: .currency)
204	        case let .unknown(t):
205	            // Only the type discriminator round-trips; unknown fields are not preserved
206	            try container.encode(MetricKind.unknown(t), forKey: .type)
207	        }
208	    }
209	}

1	import AppKit
2	import SwiftUI
3	import AIUsagesTrackersLib
4	import AppIconKit
5	
6	@MainActor
7	final class AppDelegate: NSObject, NSApplicationDelegate {
8	    private var poller: UsagePoller?
9	    private var accountMonitor: ClaudeActiveAccountMonitor?
10	    private var pidGuard: AppPidGuard?
11	    private var usageStore: UsageStore?
12	    private var refreshState: RefreshState?
13	    private var statusItem: NSStatusItem?
14	    private var popover: NSPopover?
15	    private var appearanceObserver: NSKeyValueObservation?
16	    private var settingsWindowObserver: NSObjectProtocol?
17	
18	    /// Shared preferences store — exposed as static so the SwiftUI Settings scene
19	    /// (constructed before applicationDidFinishLaunching) can access it.
20	    static let sharedPreferences: UserDefaultsAppPreferences = UserDefaultsAppPreferences()
21	
22	    /// Shared usage store — populated in applicationDidFinishLaunching. The Settings
23	    /// scene is instantiated before the store exists, so it must read through this
24	    /// mutable holder. Nil until the first launch callback has run.
25	    static var sharedStore: UsageStore?
26	
27	    func applicationDidFinishLaunching(_ notification: Notification) {
28	        NSApplication.shared.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)
29	
30	        // No .app bundle ships with this SwiftPM executable, so AppKit has no
31	        // static icon slot — set the Dock / Cmd+Tab icon programmatically.
32	        if let icon = AppIconRenderer.makeImage(pixelSize: 1024) {
33	            NSApplication.shared.applicationIconImage = icon
34	        }
35	
36	        let home = FileManager.default.homeDirectoryForCurrentUser.path
37	        let guard_ = AppPidGuard(cacheDir: "\(home)/.cache/ai-usages-tracker")
38	        do {
39	            try guard_.acquire()
40	        } catch AppPidGuardError.alreadyRunning(let pid, _) {
41	            let alert = NSAlert()
42	            alert.messageText = "AI Usages Tracker is already running"
43	            alert.informativeText = "Another instance is active (PID \(pid)). Use the menu bar icon to quit it before launching a new one."
44	            alert.runModal()
45	            NSApplication.shared.terminate(nil)
46	            return
47	        } catch {
48	            let alert = NSAlert()
49	            alert.messageText = "Failed to start AI Usages Tracker"
50	            alert.informativeText = error.localizedDescription
51	            alert.runModal()
52	            NSApplication.shared.terminate(nil)
53	            return
54	        }
55	        pidGuard = guard_
56	
57	        Loggers.setPreferences(Self.sharedPreferences)
58	
59	        MenuBarSegmentsSeeder.seedIfNeeded(preferences: Self.sharedPreferences)
60	
61	        // Reconcile launch-at-login preference with system state — the user may
62	        // have disabled the entry in System Settings > Login Items directly.
63	        let launchService = LaunchAtLoginService.shared
64	        if Self.sharedPreferences.launchAtLogin != launchService.isEnabled {
65	            Self.sharedPreferences.launchAtLogin = launchService.isEnabled
66	        }
67	
68	        let fileManager = UsagesFileManager.shared
69	        let refreshState = RefreshState()
70	        let poller = UsagePoller(
71	            connectors: [ClaudeCodeConnector()],
72	            statusConnectors: [ClaudeStatusConnector()],
73	            fileManager: fileManager,
74	            refreshState: refreshState,
75	            preferences: Self.sharedPreferences
76	        )
77	        let accountMonitor = ClaudeActiveAccountMonitor(
78	            fileManager: fileManager,
79	            onActiveAccountChanged: { [weak poller] _ in
80	                await poller?.pollOnce(force: true)
81	            }
82	        )
83	        self.poller = poller
84	        self.accountMonitor = accountMonitor
85	        self.refreshState = refreshState
86	
87	        let usagesPath = fileManager.filePath
88	        let fileWatcher = UsagesFileWatcher(path: usagesPath)
89	        let store = UsageStore(fileWatcher: fileWatcher, preferences: Self.sharedPreferences)
90	        self.usageStore = store
91	        Self.sharedStore = store
92	
93	        setupStatusItem(store: store, refreshState: refreshState)
94	        trackStoreChanges(store: store)
95	
96	        Task {
97	            store.start()
98	            await poller.start()
99	            await accountMonitor.start()
100	        }
101	    }
102	
103	    func applicationWillTerminate(_ notification: Notification) {
104	        quit()
105	    }
106	
107	    // MARK: - Status item
108	
109	    private func setupStatusItem(store: UsageStore, refreshState: RefreshState) {
110	        let item = NSStatusBar.system.statusItem(withLength: NSStatusItem.variableLength)
111	        statusItem = item
112	
113	        let popover = NSPopover()
114	        popover.behavior = .transient
115	        let hosting = NSHostingController(
116	            rootView: UsageDetailsView(
117	                store: store,
118	                refreshState: refreshState,
119	                onRefresh: { [weak self] in
120	                    await self?.poller?.pollOnce(now: Date(), force: true)
121	                },
122	                onOpenSettings: { [weak self] in
123	                    self?.openSettings()
124	                },
125	                onQuit: { [weak self] in
126	                    self?.quit()
127	                }
128	            )
129	        )
130	        // Let SwiftUI's intrinsic size drive the popover so it grows with content
131	        // up to the screen-ratio cap enforced inside UsageDetailsView.
132	        hosting.sizingOptions = [.preferredContentSize]
133	        popover.contentViewController = hosting
134	        self.popover = popover
135	
136	        if let button = item.button {
137	            button.target = self
138	            button.action = #selector(togglePopover(_:))
139	            // The menu bar appearance can change (wallpaper-driven tinting) without
140	            // the app's effectiveAppearance changing, so we observe the button itself.
141	            appearanceObserver = button.observe(\.effectiveAppearance, options: [.new]) { [weak self] _, _ in
142	                Task { @MainActor in self?.refreshStatusItemImage() }
143	            }
144	        }
145	
146	        refreshStatusItemImage()
147	    }
148	
149	    private func refreshStatusItemImage() {
150	        guard let store = usageStore, let button = statusItem?.button else { return }
151	        let isDark = button.effectiveAppearance.bestMatch(from: [.darkAqua, .aqua]) == .darkAqua
152	        let image = MenuBarLabelRenderer.render(
153	            segments: store.menuBarSegments,
154	            fallbackText: store.menuBarText,
155	            isDarkMenuBar: isDark
156	        )
157	        button.image = image
158	    }
159	
160	    @objc private func togglePopover(_ sender: Any?) {
161	        guard let popover, let button = statusItem?.button else { return }
162	        if popover.isShown {
163	            popover.performClose(sender)
164	        } else {
165	            popover.show(relativeTo: button.bounds, of: button, preferredEdge: .minY)
166	            popover.contentViewController?.view.window?.makeKey()
167	        }
168	    }
169	
170	    // MARK: - Observation
171	
172	    /// Re-arms `withObservationTracking` after every change so the status item
173	    /// keeps mirroring the store. Tracking fires exactly once per registration.
174	    private func trackStoreChanges(store: UsageStore) {
175	        withObservationTracking {
176	            _ = store.menuBarSegments
177	            _ = store.menuBarText
178	        } onChange: { [weak self] in
179	            Task { @MainActor in
180	                guard let self, let store = self.usageStore else { return }
181	                self.refreshStatusItemImage()
182	                self.trackStoreChanges(store: store)
183	            }
184	        }
185	    }
186	
187	    // MARK: - Settings
188	
189	    private func openSettings() {
190	        popover?.performClose(nil)
191	        // .accessory apps have no dock presence; showSettingsWindow: silently fails
192	        // unless we temporarily switch to .regular so AppKit can make the window key.
193	        NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.regular)
194	        NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)
195	
196	        // SwiftUI creates the Settings window lazily on first showSettingsWindow: —
197	        // if it already exists, reuse it directly to avoid responder-chain races
198	        // that occur during the popover-dismissal + activation-policy transition.
199	        if let settingsWindow = Self.findSettingsWindow() {
200	            settingsWindow.makeKeyAndOrderFront(nil)
201	        } else {
202	            // First-time path: AppKit needs a runloop tick after the .accessory →
203	            // .regular transition to install the standard App menu (which is where
204	            // SwiftUI wires the Settings… action). sendAction with a nil target
205	            // relies on that wiring, so we trigger the menu item directly once
206	            // AppKit has had a chance to build it.
207	            DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 0.15) {
208	                Self.triggerSettingsMenuItem()
209	                if let settingsWindow = Self.findSettingsWindow() {
210	                    settingsWindow.makeKeyAndOrderFront(nil)
211	                }
212	            }
213	        }
214	
215	        // Restore .accessory policy once the settings window is closed.
216	        if settingsWindowObserver == nil {
217	            settingsWindowObserver = NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(
218	                forName: NSWindow.willCloseNotification,
219	                object: nil,
220	                queue: .main
221	            ) { [weak self] notification in
222	                guard let self else { return }
223	                // Only react to the Settings window closing — popovers and other
224	                // transient windows also emit willClose.
225	                guard let closingWindow = notification.object as? NSWindow else { return }
226	                let windowIdentifier = closingWindow.identifier?.rawValue ?? ""
227	                guard windowIdentifier.contains("Settings") || windowIdentifier.contains("Preferences") else { return }
228	                // willClose fires while the window is still visible and before
229	                // AppKit tears it down; defer to the next runloop tick so the
230	                // activation-policy change isn't overridden by pending AppKit work.
231	                DispatchQueue.main.async {
232	                    NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)
233	                    // Accessory transition doesn't always remove the app from the
234	                    // Cmd+Tab / app-switcher list until another app is activated.
235	                    if let frontmost = NSWorkspace.shared.runningApplications.first(where: {
236	                        $0.isActive && $0.processIdentifier != ProcessInfo.processInfo.processIdentifier
237	                    }) {
238	                        frontmost.activate()
239	                    } else {
240	                        NSApp.hide(nil)
241	                    }
242	                    if let token = self.settingsWindowObserver {
243	                        NotificationCenter.default.removeObserver(token)
244	                        self.settingsWindowObserver = nil
245	                    }
246	                }
247	            }
248	        }
249	    }
250	
251	    /// SwiftUI tags its Settings window with an identifier containing "Settings"
252	    /// (or "Preferences" on older SDKs). Match on the stable identifier rather
253	    /// than localized titles.
254	    private static func findSettingsWindow() -> NSWindow? {
255	        NSApp.windows.first { window in
256	            let identifier = window.identifier?.rawValue ?? ""
257	            return identifier.contains("Settings") || identifier.contains("Preferences")
258	        }
259	    }
260	
261	    /// Walk the main menu looking for the App menu's Settings… (or Preferences…)
262	    /// item and invoke it. Going through the menu guarantees the action is
263	    /// routed to whichever responder SwiftUI wired up, instead of relying on
264	    /// `sendAction(_:to:from:)` to resolve a nil target in the responder chain
265	    /// — which is unreliable right after an activation-policy switch.
266	    private static func triggerSettingsMenuItem() {
267	        guard let mainMenu = NSApp.mainMenu else { return }
268	        for topItem in mainMenu.items {
269	            guard let submenu = topItem.submenu else { continue }
270	            for (index, item) in submenu.items.enumerated() {
271	                let title = item.title

3	public actor ClaudeStatusConnector: StatusConnector {
4	    nonisolated public let vendor: Vendor = .claude
5	
6	    private let logger: FileLogger
7	    private let session: URLSession
8	    private let endpointURLString: String
9	
10	    private static let requestTimeoutSeconds: TimeInterval = 5
11	    public static let defaultEndpoint = "https://status.anthropic.com/api/v2/incidents/unresolved.json"
12	
13	    public init(
14	        logger: FileLogger = Loggers.claude,
15	        session: URLSession = .shared,
16	        endpointURLString: String = ClaudeStatusConnector.defaultEndpoint
17	    ) {
18	        self.logger = logger
19	        self.session = session
20	        self.endpointURLString = endpointURLString
21	    }
22	
23	    public func fetchOutages() async throws -> [Outage] {
24	        guard let url = URL(string: endpointURLString) else {
25	            throw StatusConnectorError.invalidURL(rawValue: endpointURLString)
26	        }
27	        var request = URLRequest(url: url)
28	        request.timeoutInterval = Self.requestTimeoutSeconds
29	        request.setValue("application/json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Accept")
30	
31	        logger.log(.debug, "Fetching Claude status from \(endpointURLString)")
32	
33	        let data: Data
34	        let response: URLResponse
35	        do {
36	            (data, response) = try await session.data(for: request)
37	        } catch {
38	            logger.log(.warning, "Status endpoint network error: \(error)")
39	            throw StatusConnectorError.networkError(underlying: error, url: endpointURLString)
40	        }
41	
42	        let httpCode = (response as? HTTPURLResponse)?.statusCode ?? -1
43	        guard httpCode == 200 else {
44	            logger.log(.warning, "Status endpoint returned HTTP \(httpCode)")
45	            throw StatusConnectorError.unexpectedResponse(statusCode: httpCode, url: endpointURLString)
46	        }
47	
48	        let decoded: StatuspageIncidentsResponse
49	        do {
50	            decoded = try JSONDecoder().decode(StatuspageIncidentsResponse.self, from: data)
51	        } catch {
52	            logger.log(.error, "Status endpoint parse error: \(error)")
53	            throw StatusConnectorError.parseError(underlying: error, url: endpointURLString)
54	        }
55	
56	        let outages: [Outage] = decoded.incidents.compactMap { incident in
57	            // "none" impact means the incident is informational with no user-facing effect —
58	            // don't surface it as an outage in the UI.
59	            guard incident.impact != "none" else { return nil }
60	            let severity = OutageSeverity(rawValue: incident.impact)
61	            // Invalid shortlink must not drop the incident itself — surface it without href.
62	            let href: URL?
63	            if let raw = incident.shortlink {
64	                href = URL(string: raw)
65	            } else {
66	                href = nil
67	            }
68	            return Outage(
69	                vendor: vendor,
70	                errorMessage: incident.name,
71	                severity: severity,
72	                since: ISODate(rawValue: incident.created_at),
73	                href: href
74	            )
75	        }
76	        logger.log(.info, "Claude status: \(outages.count) active outage(s)")
77	        return outages
78	    }
79	
80	    // MARK: - DTOs
81	
82	    private struct StatuspageIncidentsResponse: Decodable {
83	        let incidents: [StatuspageIncident]
84	    }
85	
86	    private struct StatuspageIncident: Decodable {
87	        let name: String
88	        let impact: String
89	        let created_at: String
90	        let shortlink: String?
91	    }
92	}

# Roadmap process

Planned work lives under `roadmap/` at the repository root. Unlike files in `docs/`, roadmap entries intentionally describe future, transient state — they are exempt from the "write for longevity" clauses of `.claude/rules/markdown-authoring.md`.

## Layout

- `roadmap/index.md` — open epics with their status and a Mermaid dependency graph. Single source of truth for ordering, dependencies, and status.
- `roadmap/<slug>.md` — one file per epic. Slug is kebab-case, English, concise.

When a feature ships, **both its epic file and its index entry are deleted**. The index only ever lists features that remain to be implemented.

## Granularity

One file equals one epic: a user-visible capability large enough to warrant a named milestone, small enough to be described on a single page. Sub-tasks of an epic stay inside its file and do not get their own roadmap entry.

## Feature file template

Each epic file follows this exact structure (omit `Notes` when empty):

```markdown
# <Epic name>

## Goal

<One or two sentences: the user problem solved.>

## Dependencies

<Bulleted list of links to other roadmap files, or the single word `None.`.>

## Scope

<Bulleted list of what is included.>

**Out of scope**

<Bulleted list of explicit boundaries.>

## Acceptance criteria

<Bulleted list of verifiable conditions.>

## Notes

<Optional. Technical constraints or architectural intent.>
```

Status does NOT appear in feature files — it lives only in `index.md` to avoid drift.

## Index format

`roadmap/index.md` contains two sections:

### Dependency graph

A Mermaid `graph TD` block listing every open epic as a node and every inter-dependency as a directed edge. Node IDs match epic slugs; labels are the epic display names. Edges point from dependency to dependent (`A --> B` means B depends on A). Isolated nodes (no dependencies among open epics) appear without edges.

```markdown
## Dependency graph

\`\`\`mermaid
graph TD
    epic-a["Epic A"]
    epic-b["Epic B"]
    epic-a --> epic-b
\`\`\`
```

### Epics list

An ordered list in topological order (dependencies first). Each entry uses this line format:

```markdown
1. `<status>` — [<Epic name>](<slug>.md) — <one-line summary>
```

Status values: `planned` | `in-progress`. Reordering or renumbering the list is how the plan is revised. The list must stay contiguous and in strict topological order.

## Creating a new epic

Use the `roadmap-feature` skill: it gathers context, checks for duplicates, writes the feature file, inserts it at the right position in the Epics list, and adds its node (and edges) to the Mermaid dependency graph.

## Closing a shipped epic

When a feature's acceptance criteria are all verified:

1. Remove the epic's line from the `## Epics` list and renumber.
2. Remove the epic's node and any edges involving it from the Mermaid graph.
3. Delete `roadmap/<slug>.md`.
4. Delete `roadmap/<slug>.plan.md` if it exists.

The `implement-roadmap-feature` Archon workflow handles all four steps automatically in its `close-feature` phase.
